headlines.title: 3 big Senate fundraising takeaways: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, we break down the toplines from a fresh batch of campaign finance filings. Plus, Jonathan Allen previews President Donald Trump’s speech tonight from the White House.

headlines.title: American woman held in Iran since 2024 released, Trump announces
headlines.description: Jared Genser, a lawyer who has been involved with high-profile cases of U.S. citizens detained in Iran, named the released citizen as his client, Dena Karari.

headlines.title: Trump teleprompter operator betting allegations
headlines.description: A longtime teleprompter operator for President Trump is on unpaid administrative leave after allegations that he profited from bets on Trump’s remarks through the prediction market Kalshi. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

headlines.title: Epstein survivor describes 'emotional' Todd Blanche meeting
headlines.description: Epstein survivor Dani Bensky describes the "emotional" meeting she and fellow survivors had with Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche. Bensky calls for continued commitment from Blanche in the Epstein investigation, describing the meeting as "frustrating."

headlines.title: Truth Social plans to sell Wall Street firms 'fast' access to president's posts
headlines.description: Truth Social plans to sell Wall Street firms 'fast' access to President Trump's posts. The product, called ‘Truth API’, will deliver posts from the 10 most influential accounts to customers at a significantly ​faster pace than a regular push notification.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Sen. John Fetterman says he’ll leave the Democratic Party if it ‘officially’ becomes ‘anti-Israel’
headlines.description: Sen. John Fetterman said he would leave the Democratic Party if it “officially” becomes “the anti-Israel party.”

headlines.title: What to know about the SAVE America Act and Trump’s push for voting changes
headlines.description: Trump is renewing his push for the SAVE America Act, a bill that would require states to look for noncitizen voters and would force voters to confirm U.S. citizenship when participating in elections.

headlines.title: Judge awards Hunter Biden $1.7 million in defamation suit against former Overstock CEO
headlines.description: Judge Stephen Wilson wrote that former Overstock CEO Patrick Byrne’s comments were “not a product of excusable neglect but rather of coordinated strategy.”

headlines.title: Democrats’ deepening Israel divisions: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, our Capitol Hill team dives into the latest disagreement among Democrats over Israel. Plus, Andrea Mitchell examines whether Ukraine can build on the advantages it has developed in the war with Russia.

headlines.title: Nearly half of House Democrats vote to cut off aid to Israel as public opinion shifts
headlines.description: More than 100 Democrats voted to end U.S. assistance. The vote sparked a rare split between the chamber’s top two Democrats: Hakeem Jeffries and Katherine Clark.

headlines.title: Indian billionaire denies U.S. investment pledge influenced DOJ to seek case dismissal
headlines.description: Federal prosecutors sought to drop the case against Gautam Adani in May, a significant reversal in a sweeping bribery and fraud investigation.
